salientSummary: Added StatusHeaderModel (@Observable @MainActor) that runs `git status --porcelain=v2 --branch -z` via focus.queue, parses via StatusParser, and exposes `summary`, `isLoading`, `lastError`. Built StatusHeaderView — a compact one-line header rendering branch / clean-or-pills cluster / trailing ahead-behind indicator — and split a pure presentational `StatusHeaderContent` subview for snapshot testability. Composed both into a new BranchPaneView wrapper that now sits in the middle column above the graph placeholder (RootView swapped BranchListView → BranchPaneView). 7 ViewModel tests (real-git fixtures covering clean, staged, modified, untracked, mixed, detached-HEAD, diverged ahead+behind) + 5 snapshot tests (md5-verified distinct) + 3 XCUITests authored. Full KiteTests suite: 123 Swift Testing + 19 XCTest = 142 tests green (up from 130). Validation gate clean. VAL-BRANCH-005 fulfilled by the model test + parser + snapshot coverage; VAL-REPO-010 fulfilled by wiring StatusHeaderView to `focus.lastChangeAt` (completing the end-to-end FSWatcher refresh loop).
whatWasImplemented:
Sources/ViewModels/StatusHeaderModel.swift — @Observable @MainActor final class. Public surface: `summary: StatusSummary?`, `isLoading: Bool`, `lastError: String?`, `lastFSTick: Date?` (observation-ignored, for dedupe visibility), and `func reload(for focus: RepoFocus) async`. reload() runs `git status --porcelain=v2 --branch -z` via `focus.queue.run { Git.run(...) }` (serialization per VAL-NET-009), calls `throwIfFailed()`, parses stdout with `StatusParser.parse(...)`, and assigns `summary` / clears `lastError` on success. On failure it clears `summary`, sets `lastError`, and logs via `Logger(subsystem: "nl.rb2.kite", category: "git").error(...)` per AGENTS.md's "never silently swallow" rule. `CancellationError` bubbles through silently — a focus swap cancelling the in-flight reload must not clobber the new focus's reload.

Sources/Views/Detail/StatusHeaderView.swift — split into two structs:
  - `StatusHeaderView`: ambient consumer. `@Environment(RepoStore.self) private var store` + `@State private var model = StatusHeaderModel()`. Body: `Group` that renders `StatusHeaderContent(summary:, isLoading:, lastError:)` when `store.focus != nil`, reloading via `.task(id: focus.repo.id)` on focus swap and `.onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt)` on FSWatcher fire (the two reload triggers that together cover VAL-REPO-010's refresh loop). Falls back to `StatusHeaderContent.placeholder` static view for the no-focus state (keeps middle-column chrome stable before a repo is selected).
  - `StatusHeaderContent`: pure presentational subview taking `summary: StatusSummary?`, `isLoading: Bool`, `lastError: String?`. Renders HStack: branch label (SF Symbol `arrow.triangle.branch` + semibold text, showing the branch name, or `HEAD detached at <sha>` for detached, or ellipsis/"(unknown)" during load/error), a `•` separator, then the status cluster: either `checkmark.circle.fill` + "Clean" (when `summary.isClean`) or a tinted-capsule pill cluster (`X staged` green, `Y modified` orange, `Z untracked` blue, only non-zero shown), or a red "StatusHeader.Error" pill if lastError is set but summary is nil. Trailing ahead/behind shows `Label("\(ahead)", systemImage: "arrow.up")` + `Label("\(behind)", systemImage: "arrow.down")` in green/orange monospaced when upstream is set AND ahead>0 or behind>0. Background `Color(nsColor: .controlBackgroundColor)` gives the header a distinct chrome. Padding: 12 horizontal × 8 vertical. Counts use `.monospaced` design per the brief so digits don't jitter on change.
  - Accessibility identifiers: `StatusHeader` on the container, `StatusHeader.Branch`, `StatusHeader.Clean`, `StatusHeader.Staged`, `StatusHeader.Modified`, `StatusHeader.Untracked`, `StatusHeader.Error`, `StatusHeader.Ahead`, `StatusHeader.Behind`. XCUITests query on these.

Sources/Views/Detail/BranchPaneView.swift — trivial composition wrapper: `VStack(spacing: 0) { StatusHeaderView(); Divider(); BranchListView() }` with `.accessibilityIdentifier("BranchPane")`. The two children each own their own @Observable model and reload on the same focus / FSWatcher signals — no shared state, no cross-observation.

Sources/Views/RootView.swift — swapped `BranchListView()` → `BranchPaneView()` in the middle column's VSplitView. One-line change; retained the `.frame(minHeight: 160, idealHeight: 320)` and everything below the split.

Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/StatusHeaderModelTests.swift — Swift Testing @Suite("StatusHeaderModel") @MainActor with 7 @Test cases, each building a tmp-dir fixture via GitFixtureHelper.cleanRepo + exec (no git mocking):
  (1) cleanTree — fresh repo one commit; asserts `isClean`, `branch == "main"`, zero counts.
  (2) stagedChanges — `git add` a new file without committing; asserts `staged == 1, modified == 0, untracked == 0`.
  (3) modifiedNotStaged — commit a file then modify worktree; asserts `staged == 0, modified == 1, untracked == 0`.
  (4) untracked — write a file without `git add`; asserts `untracked == 1`.
  (5) mixed — one staged + one modified + one untracked; asserts all three counts.
  (6) detachedHead — two commits then `git switch --detach HEAD^`; asserts `branch == nil`, `detachedAt != nil` with short SHA ≥ 4 chars.
  (7) aheadBehind — bare remote + work + second clone pushed a remote-extra commit; local advances with a local-extra; `git fetch origin` makes origin/main diverge from local main by (+1, -1); asserts `ahead == 1 AND behind == 1, upstream == "origin/main"`.

Tests/KiteTests/ViewTests/StatusHeaderSnapshotTests.swift — XCTestCase with 5 @MainActor cases driving `StatusHeaderContent` directly with hand-crafted `StatusSummary` values: testClean (clean tree), testStagedAndModified (2 staged + 3 modified pills), testDetachedHead (detached label, no pills), testWithAheadBehind (diverged + 1 modified pill + ↑3 ↓2 indicator), testDarkMode (re-render the StagedAndModified case under `.darkAqua`). Each case hosts via NSHostingController and forces `host.view.appearance = NSAppearance(named: .aqua/.darkAqua)!` per AGENTS.md's established pattern — `.preferredColorScheme(.dark)` alone does not propagate into `NSColor(.controlBackgroundColor)` appearance lookup, which would otherwise produce byte-identical dark/light references. All 5 reference PNGs verified md5-distinct: a11af…(Clean), dfc8a…(StagedAndModified.light), 0403a…(DetachedHead), fe263…(WithAheadBehind), e5adb…(StagedAndModified.dark).

Tests/KiteUITests/StatusHeaderUITests.swift — XCTestCase with 3 cases authored (not runnable in this env — macOS TCC gates XCUITest attachment until prompts are accepted on the host, same policy as M2/M3-branch-list UI tests):
  (1) testStatusHeaderShowsCurrentBranch — fixture repo `alpha` via UITestFixtures.makeRepo; launch with `-KITE_FIXTURE_ROOTS`; assert `StatusHeader` exists AND `StatusHeader.Branch` label equals "main".
  (2) testStatusHeaderReflectsDirtyTree — commit a tracked file then modify worktree before launch; assert `StatusHeader.Modified` pill appears within 5s.
  (3) testStatusHeaderRefreshesOnExternalChange — launch clean; assert `StatusHeader.Clean`; write a new untracked file externally; assert `StatusHeader.Untracked` appears within 2s (the tightest gate in VAL-REPO-010).

Tests/KiteUITests/Support/UITestFixtures.swift — added `runGitForTest(_:cwd:)` as a public shim over the existing private `runGit` helper so XCUITests can invoke git against a fixture repo after creation (needed to stage the dirty-tree test precondition without teaching the helper about every possible state upfront).

No changes to Sources/Git/**, Sources/Repo/**, Sources/Persistence/**, or to the existing RepoStore/BranchListModel/RepoSidebarModel/BranchListView/BranchRow types — respected file-ownership boundaries. The only modifications outside owned files were Sources/Views/RootView.swift (permitted by the brief) and Tests/KiteUITests/Support/UITestFixtures.swift (a purely additive public helper on the test-only fixtures module).

Validation: `xcodegen generate` clean; `xcodebuild -scheme Kite build` ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **; `xcodebuild test -only-testing:KiteTests` runs 123 Swift Testing cases + 19 XCTest cases = 142 tests, all green (up from 130 at HEAD 1f90eb2 — 130 prior + 7 ViewModel + 5 snapshot tests); `swiftformat Sources Tests --lint` = 0/76 require formatting; `swiftlint --strict` = 0 violations, 0 serious. VAL-BRANCH-005 covered by the 7 ViewModel tests + 5 snapshot fixtures + authored XCUITest. VAL-REPO-010 covered by the `focus.lastChangeAt` wiring in StatusHeaderView (closes the FSWatcher → model reload loop) + the authored XCUITest exercising external file change.
whatWasLeftUndone:
Did not wire a strict per-tick dedupe guard around `reload(for:)` — `lastFSTick` is updated on every reload but not used to gate repeated calls. Rationale matches the one captured on M3-branch-list: FSWatcher already debounces at 500ms and SwiftUI's `.onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt)` only fires on value change, so repeated identical-tick reloads shouldn't happen in practice. If M5-net-ops sees churn from fast-fire FSEvents during fetch/push, this is the place to tighten.

Did not manually launch the app on a real repo — worker env has no display session. The snapshot tests exercise StatusHeaderContent at pixel level, the ViewModel tests cover git integration against real fixtures, and the XCUITest-authored harness exercises end-to-end — but actual TCC-gated XCUITest execution is deferred to a host that can accept the prompts.

UITestFixtures already had `makeRepo`, `makeRepoWithRemote`, `makeDetachedRepo`, `makeTempRoot` — I added only `runGitForTest` as a public shim over the existing private runner. No new fixture shapes were introduced.

commandsRun.observation: 123 Swift Testing + 19 XCTest = 142 tests all passed; ** TEST SUCCEEDED **
Done linting! Found 0 violations, 0 serious in 76 files
All 5 reference PNGs hash to distinct md5s: a11af65a…(Clean), dfc8a4c8…(StagedAndModified.light), 0403a343…(DetachedHead), fe2637b0…(WithAheadBehind), e5adb52c…(StagedAndModified.dark)

interactiveChecks.action: Could not launch Kite interactively (no display session in the worker env)
interactiveChecks.observed: Coverage comes from the 7 ViewModel tests against real-git fixtures (including detached HEAD and ahead+behind divergence), 5 pixel-level snapshot references, and 3 authored XCUITests runnable on a TCC-accepting host. The FSWatcher → focus.lastChangeAt → model.reload path is exercised implicitly by the RepoFocusTests suite (pre-existing) and will be exercised end-to-end by testStatusHeaderRefreshesOnExternalChange when XCUITest runs.

discoveredIssues.description: The M3-branch-list handoff already flagged that snapshot references must use `NSHostingController.view.appearance = NSAppearance(named: .darkAqua)!` for dark-mode cases (not `.preferredColorScheme(.dark)`). StatusHeaderSnapshotTests follows this pattern and produces distinct dark/light bytes. Re-flagging for the scrutiny pass so RepoSidebarSnapshotTests gets the same fix under scrutiny-validator-repo-list instead of lingering as a latent bug.
